skill.name: scaffold-exercises
skill.description: Scaffold course exercise folders with section, problem, solution, and explainer variants, then validate the structure with the project linter.
skill.summary: Create lint-ready exercise directory structures for course content.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown inline command examples and three weak-cryptography indicators. The weak-cryptography indicators are false positives from ordinary documentation text, while the external command instructions are real but aligned with the skill purpose. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, obfuscation, or data exfiltration evidence was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Required for Scaffolding
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to create directories, run a project linter, use git move commands, and commit changes. These commands are expected for exercise scaffolding, but they modify the local repository and depend on plan-derived paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command instructions are explicit in the Markdown and match the static external_commands detections. They appear legitimate for this skill, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts at the documented lines are caused by normal prose and Markdown examples. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher configuration, or security-sensitive implementation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines contain description text or a Markdown readme stub, not cryptographic code. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptography use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository-Mutating Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command examples and workflow steps for mkdir, git mv, git commit, and pnpm lint. Agents should verify paths and repository state before executing these commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly instruct command execution that can change filesystem and git state. No evidence shows network access, secret access, or malicious payloads.

content.value_statement: Course exercise repositories need consistent folder names, variants, and readme files to pass validation. This skill turns a section plan into lint-ready exercise scaffolding with problem, solution, and explainer folders.
actual_capabilities: Parses course plans into numbered sections and exercise directories.
Creates explainer, problem, and solution variant folders when requested.
Adds minimal readme files so new exercise folders are not empty.
Applies the repository naming convention for sections and exercises.
Runs the project exercise linter and iterates on structural errors.
Uses git-aware moves when renumbering or relocating exercises.
limitations: It assumes the target repository uses the documented exercise folder convention.
It does not design full lesson content beyond basic scaffolding unless requested.
It requires local command execution for linting and git operations.
It does not validate educational quality or technical correctness of future exercise content.

use_cases.title: Create a New Course Section
use_cases.description: Convert a planned module into numbered exercise folders with explainer stubs.

use_cases.title: Add Practice Variants
use_cases.description: Add problem and solution folders for exercises that need hands-on coding work.

use_cases.title: Renumber Existing Exercises
use_cases.description: Move exercises into a new order while preserving git history and lint compatibility.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold One Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new section with default explainer folders.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create section 05 named Memory Skill Building with three explainer exercises: Introduction to Memory, Short-term Memory, and Long-term Memory.

prompt_templates.title: Add Exercise Variants
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with explicit problem and solution variants.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold section 03 Retrieval with exercises 03.01 Query Basics as explainer and 03.02 BM25 Ranking with explainer, problem, and solution folders.

prompt_templates.title: Validate New Scaffolding
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that includes lint verification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the exercise folders from this plan, add minimal readmes, run the exercise linter, and fix any structure errors it reports.

prompt_templates.title: Reorganize a Course Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that requires git-aware moves and renumbering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Renumber the retrieval section so embeddings becomes exercise 01.04, preserve git history for moved folders, update prefixes, and rerun lint.

output_examples.input: Create a Memory Skill Building section with three exercises.
output: A new numbered section folder is created under exercises.
Each exercise receives an explainer folder and a minimal readme title.
The linter is run and structural issues are corrected.

output_examples.input: Add problem and solution variants for one exercise.
output: The exercise folder includes explainer, problem, and solution variants.
Each variant has a non-empty readme file.
The structure follows the repository naming rules.

output_examples.input: Move an exercise to a new number in the same section.
output: The folder is moved with git-aware commands.
The numeric prefix is updated to match the new order.
Lint validation is repeated after the move.

best_practices: Provide exact section numbers, exercise numbers, and variant types before scaffolding.
Run the exercise linter after every create, move, or rename operation.
Review generated readme stubs before adding full instructional content.
anti_patterns: Do not create unnumbered or mixed-case exercise directory names.
Do not leave primary variant readme files empty.
Do not use plain file moves when preserving git history matters.

faq.question: What repository layout does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It expects an exercises directory with numbered section folders and numbered exercise folders.

faq.question: Can it create problem and solution folders?
faq.answer: Yes. It creates problem, solution, and explainer variants when the plan requests them.

faq.question: Does it write complete lesson content?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on folder structure and minimal readme stubs unless more content is requested.

faq.question: Why does the skill run shell commands?
faq.answer: It needs local commands to create folders, move files with git history, and run the project linter.

faq.question: Is this safe for any repository?
faq.answer: No. Use it only in repositories that follow the documented exercise convention.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
